leader_proposals.pattern_description: Sustained pattern of reactive, escalatory threats with rapid deadline revisions. Trump issued repeated threats to destroy Iranian power plants (Apr 3), extended Iran deadline for Hormuz reopening on short notice (Mar 26), and issued ultimatums framed as negotiation tactics ('Make A Deal Before It's Too Late'). Pattern shows reactive response to tactical developments (downed F-35 report) rather than deliberative strategy.

evidence_chain.headline: Trump threatens to destroy Iran power plants as reports emerge of downed U.S. F-35
evidence_chain.relevance: Reactive threat issued in response to specific military incident, exemplifies impulsive escalation

evidence_chain.headline: Trump Warns US 'Hasn't Even Started' To Destroy Iran, Says Regime Must Act 'Fast'
evidence_chain.relevance: Hyperbolic threat language designed for immediate pressure, not calibrated strategic communication

evidence_chain.headline: Trump extends Iran deadline for Hormuz reopening to April 6 citing ongoing negotiations
evidence_chain.relevance: Demonstrates pattern of deadline flexibility driven by tactical developments rather than strategic plan

evidence_chain.headline: "Make A Deal Before It's Too Late": Trump Threatens Tehran (Again)
evidence_chain.relevance: Repeated threat framing suggests reactive escalation pattern rather than one-time decision

leader_proposals.counter_evidence: Score already at 90/100; threshold for change is ±2.8 points requiring 4+ items and sustained pattern. While evidence supports current high score, insufficient delta movement justifies proposal. Current score appears calibrated to observed behavior.

leader_proposals.pattern_description: Systematic evasion of ICC accountability mechanisms through deliberate legal delays and international avoidance. Netanyahu skipped Davos 2026 specifically to avoid ICC warrant execution (Jan 20), delayed ICJ evidence submission using classified war crimes defense (Jan 4), and further delayed ICC trial to March 2026 (Mar 12). Pattern demonstrates active dismantling of accountability through legal proceduralism and jurisdictional avoidance.

evidence_chain.headline: Netanyahu skips Davos 2026 to avoid ICC arrest warrant execution in Switzerland
evidence_chain.relevance: Direct evidence of deliberate avoidance of accountability mechanism (ICC warrant) through strategic location avoidance

evidence_chain.headline: Israeli legal officials delay ICJ evidence submission to March 12 amid classified war crimes defense
evidence_chain.relevance: Systemic use of legal proceduralism and classification to delay accountability proceedings

evidence_chain.headline: Netanyahu ICC Trial Delayed Until March 2026 Amid War
evidence_chain.relevance: Further evidence of deliberate trial delay strategy, framed as war-related necessity

leader_proposals.counter_evidence: No counter-evidence of Netanyahu engaging accountability mechanisms or accepting legal proceedings voluntarily. Pattern shows consistent avoidance across multiple jurisdictions (ICC, ICJ).

leader_proposals.pattern_description: Intensified primary reliance on survival-driven tactical decisions through military straits control and regime cohesion maintenance amid external pressure. Khamenei established Larak Island 'toll booth' for Hormuz control (Apr 2), vowed to keep Strait closed as leverage (Mar 12), threatened to expand strikes to Bab el-Mandeb (Apr 1), and implemented internet blackout (35+ days by Apr 1) to suppress internal dissent while facing external military threat. Every major decision prioritizes physical/political survival over any other objective.

evidence_chain.headline: Iran Establishes Larak Island 'Toll Booth' to Control Strait of Hormuz Shipping
evidence_chain.relevance: Strategic infrastructure decision explicitly designed for survival leverage in asymmetric conflict

evidence_chain.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed as leverage against US and Israel
evidence_chain.relevance: Explicit survival-driven strategy: using economic leverage as deterrent to military threat

evidence_chain.headline: Iran Threatens to Expand Strikes to Bab el-Mandeb Strait
evidence_chain.relevance: Escalatory survival tactic: expanding threat scope to increase deterrence value

evidence_chain.headline: Iran's Internet Blackout Enters 35th Day with 1% Connectivity
evidence_chain.relevance: Information control as survival mechanism: suppressing internal dissent while regime faces external military pressure

evidence_chain.headline: Tehran Protests Against New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Continue
evidence_chain.relevance: Evidence of internal regime cohesion threat that justifies information control and survival-first decision-making

leader_proposals.counter_evidence: Zarif ceasefire proposal (Apr 4) represents minor divergence, but sourced from Foreign Affairs (western publication) and attributed to Zarif not Khamenei, suggesting regime-level survival priority overrides diplomatic overtures.

leader_proposals.pattern_description: Deliberate circumvention of War Powers Resolution accountability requirements through deadline extension tactics. Trump administration approached 60-day War Powers deadline (Apr 2) without congressional authorization framework, then extended Iran deadline to Apr 6 while maintaining military operations, effectively bypassing formal congressional constraint mechanism that requires reporting/justification.

evidence_chain.headline: Trump Administration Approaches 60-Day War Powers Resolution Deadline on Iran
evidence_chain.relevance: Trump administration facing formal accountability requirement but no evidence of pre-emptive authorization or compliance submission

evidence_chain.headline: Trump extends Iran deadline for Hormuz reopening to April 6 citing ongoing negotiations
evidence_chain.relevance: Deadline extension used to defer accountability mechanism trigger, framed as negotiation necessity

leader_proposals.counter_evidence: Threshold requires 3+ items and 2+ source blocs. Only 2 items identified; insufficient evidence for sustained pattern of deliberate bypass vs. standard executive war powers assertion.

leader_proposals.pattern_description: Elevated narcissistic positioning through Pakistan's emergence as key Iran-US mediator, displacing India as previously marginalized regional power. Munir/Pakistan military leveraged geopolitical opportunity to position themselves as indispensable mediator, potentially for regime credibility and validation.

evidence_chain.headline: India Marginalized as Pakistan Emerges as Key Iran-US Mediator
evidence_chain.relevance: Direct evidence of Pakistan military (Munir's domain) achieving elevated status and centrality in major geopolitical crisis

evidence_chain.headline: Israel kills IRGC Navy commander Alireza Tangsiri in overnight strike confirmed by Pakistan mediator
evidence_chain.relevance: Pakistan military (Munir) positioned as authoritative verifier of major military development, demonstrating elevated validation-seeking role

leader_proposals.counter_evidence: Threshold requires 3+ items with 2+ source blocs. Only 2 items and single source bloc (western). Insufficient for sustained pattern proposal.

variable_proposals.current_assessment: Iran has established de facto control point at Larak Island; Hormuz closure threatened but not fully implemented; 35+ day internet blackout suggests control measures active
variable_proposals.rationale: Iran established Larak Island 'toll booth' for Hormuz shipping control (Apr 2), explicitly vowed to keep Strait closed as leverage (Mar 12), and threatened to expand closure to Bab el-Mandeb (Apr 1). These represent sustained escalation from threats to operational infrastructure. Internet blackout (35+ days) supports enforcement capability through regime information control.

evidence_chain.headline: Iran Establishes Larak Island 'Toll Booth' to Control Strait of Hormuz Shipping
evidence_chain.relevance: Direct evidence of operational control infrastructure, not just rhetorical threat

evidence_chain.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei vows to keep Strait of Hormuz closed as leverage against US and Israel
evidence_chain.relevance: Leadership commitment to sustained closure policy for strategic leverage

evidence_chain.headline: Iran Threatens to Expand Strikes to Bab el-Mandeb Strait
evidence_chain.relevance: Escalation beyond Hormuz suggests systematic strait-control strategy expansion

variable_proposals.current_assessment: Iran's highly enriched uranium location unverified by IAEA amid underground facility expansion (Mar 31); nuclear response rhetoric from Saudi Arabia (Mar 15) indicates perceived escalation threshold
variable_proposals.rationale: IAEA unable to verify location of Iran's highly enriched uranium due to underground facility concealment (Mar 31). Mohammed bin Salman reiterated nuclear response to Iran threat (Mar 15), indicating perceived proximity to nuclear threshold. US strikes targeted 8,000 Iranian military targets including 130 vessels (Apr 4), raising nuclear signalling from both sides.

evidence_chain.relevance: Iran's concealment of nuclear material location increases uncertainty and signalling risk

variable_proposals.current_assessment: Pakistan emerging as key mediator (displacing India); Zarif proposes ceasefire in Foreign Affairs (Apr 4); direct US-Iran negotiation dynamics unclear but deadline extensions suggest ongoing communication
variable_proposals.rationale: Pakistan emerged as key Iran-US mediator (Apr 3), confirmed to have verified military developments (Mar 26). Mohammad Javad Zarif published ceasefire proposal in Foreign Affairs (Apr 4) suggesting regime willingness to signal diplomatic off-ramp. Trump extended deadline to Apr 6 citing 'ongoing negotiations' (Mar 26), indicating sustained if fragile diplomatic contact.

variable_proposals.current_assessment: 35+ day internet blackout with 1% connectivity suggests severe internal control measures; ongoing Tehran protests against Mojtaba Khamenei indicate legitimacy challenge; IRGC sanctions-evasion network disrupted by UAE arrests
variable_proposals.rationale: Internet blackout now in 35th day with only 1% connectivity (Apr 1), indicating severe regime control mechanisms required to suppress dissent. Ongoing Tehran protests against new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (Mar 8) demonstrate active legitimacy challenge. IRGC-linked sanctions-evasion money changers arrested in UAE (Apr 4) disrupts regime financial networks. Combined evidence suggests regime cohesion under severe stress.

evidence_chain.relevance: Extreme information control measure required to suppress internal dissent during military crisis

ICC warrant remains active and enforceable; jeopardy status unchanged fundamentally.

variable_proposals.current_assessment: Trump administration approached 60-day War Powers deadline (Apr 2) without apparent authorization framework; deadline extension tactics suggest circumvention of formal congressional oversight mechanism
variable_proposals.rationale: Trump administration facing War Powers Resolution accountability deadline (Apr 2) with no evidence of pre-emptive congressional authorization or formal reporting submission. Deadline extension to Apr 6 (Mar 26) frames continued military operations as negotiation necessity, effectively deferring formal congressional constraint mechanism. Pattern suggests erosion of War Powers constraint effectiveness.

no_change_assessment: Trump's narcissism (92) and impulsivity (90) scores are already at ceiling and fully supported by observed behavior; insufficient delta movement to justify proposal despite sustained pattern evidence. Modi, Putin, Xi, Erdogan, and Zelensky show insufficient sustained pattern evidence in this period to meet proposal thresholds. Narendra Modi's marginalization (Apr 3) represents single event insufficient for narcissism change proposal.
